scores.note: candidate_floor > head_floor because HEAD's body carries TWO forbidden coercion cheats (do-while(0) scheduling-fence wrapper + dead `rx = vx*cos + (uninit vz)*sin >> 12` reading vz before its proper assignment). Both are no-new-park-categories cheats-by-spelling. The clean candidate is the honest pure-C baseline; the next session should resume from it, not from HEAD.

sessions.lever: structural variant sweep (14 forms) + RTL diagnosis
directed permuter campaign (orchestrator modality change, user-authorized) — ~43k iters, 4 workers, from clean candidate.c base
sessions.evidence: permuter/cpu_get_dist workspace (base.c=cgd_iso.c, target.o at offset 0). Found a permuter-score-0 (byte-match) form BUT it is a CHEAT-FORM — REJECTED per cheats-by-any-spelling. The 0-form's closing constructs: (1) `rz++; rz--;` dead no-op pair inside the `if (v48<0)` block (extends rz live range — zero semantic purpose, same intent as HEAD's original liveness cheats); (2) `s32 vx;` declared but unused (load inlined); (3) `new_var=(u8*)a0;` alias used for ONLY the 2nd store (asymmetric drop-param-alias). Clean forms plateaued at permuter-~310 (output-310-1/), none reached 0. KEY: target's allocation IS reachable but only via rz-liveness coercion in the mutation space explored — no natural C use of rz exists in the v48 block. The clean sandbox floor stays 21.

reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is plain pure C — no asm, no pins, no dead stores, no degenerate wrappers, no volatile coercion. All 14 sweep variants scored 21 with the same clean-policy shape.

remaining_gap.summary: sched1 (sched.c:2399 rank_for_schedule) schedules `lw vx` BEFORE `lh cos_val` in the post-call basic block. The class-tie-break (class 3 'independent of last_scheduled' beats class 1 'data-dependent') picks lw vx after the sll(cos_idx). This delays the lh until after lw vx commits to $a0, so vx cannot reuse $v1 (the freshly-dead cos_idx scratch reg). The cascade shifts vx ($v1 -> $a0), cos_val ($a1 -> $a2), and downstream pseudos by one register position. Target's allocation requires lh BEFORE lw vx, which requires either (a) lh having strictly higher INSN_PRIORITY (longer chain to exit) — equal in all measured forms; or (b) lh being class 3 not class 1 — only possible if last_scheduled is NOT the sll(cos_idx) — would require restructuring so the addiu/andi/sll cos_idx chain is broken by an independent insn from outside the cos_idx chain that itself doesn't read $v1 (no natural-C structure produces this without a coercion).
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD currently uses: cpu_get_dist: $4 <-> $3 @ 8-9, @ 11, @ 14 (three register-name swaps on cos_idx scratch) + reorder 11,13,14,15,10,16,17,12 @ 10-17. These post-process the sched1 output to look like target. A pure-C close would retire ALL four.

next_hypotheses: Permuter run with directed mode (PERM_REORDER, PERM_TYPECAST) from this candidate as base — vanilla permuter on a pin-free base might surface a structural mutation the manual sweep missed.
Try declaring single_game_getEnemyCharId return type as something narrower (s16 instead of s32) — would change angle's RTL shape and may bias sched1 differently.

Investigate whether maspsx is hiding a load-delay slot opportunity that target had: target's `lh $a1, %lo(Judge)($at)` followed by `lw $v1, ...` has the lw IN the lh's load-delay slot. Our build emits the same pair but maspsx inserts a nop. Check whether a maspsx-level adjustment (per-function maspsx_label_nop opt-out?) would let sched1's natural inverse ordering still match — though this would only matter if our sched1 PRODUCED the target ordering, which it doesn't.

Read sched.c rank_for_schedule's class definitions more carefully and check whether an additional REG_DEP_LINK between lh and lw (impossible naturally) is the only path, or whether the priority calculation has a tunable input (cos_val's REF_COUNT — 4 in target, 4 in mine — but priority may not match).

rejected_forms.form: v1_clean (cos+vx+sin+vz+rx+rz+v48 single-statement decls in source order)
rejected_forms.reason: baseline natural form; sched1 hoists lw vx into cos_val region
LUID reordering doesn't change sched1's class-3 preference
intermediate naming doesn't restructure live ranges enough
vx's lower LUID doesn't override class-tie-break

rejected_forms.form: v5_struct_access (K&R top-of-block decls, identical to HEAD layout minus the cheats)
rejected_forms.reason: K&R-style decl alone gives no coercion effect

rejected_forms.form: v6_cross (named partial products before the rx/rz sums)
rejected_forms.reason: explicit naming doesn't change combined-mult shape
same as v5; no behavior change
neg_vx alias doesn't affect sched1's decision on lw vx
Judge pointer materialization is folded back by GCC; identical RTL
split scratch locals don't affect the lh-vs-lw choice

rejected_forms.form: v11_cossin_first (cos+sin declared together, then vx, vz)
rejected_forms.reason: later-LUID vx still wins class-3 over class-1 lh
array-vs-cast emits identical RTL
base alias folded by GCC; identical RTL
addiu -0xC00 changes RTL constant; combine.c folds back to addiu +0x400 OR sched1 differs; either way regresses one insn

rejected_forms.form: v16_late_v48 (v48 in inner scope after both stores)
rejected_forms.reason: scope wrap does not affect sched1 (no LOOP_BEG note generated)
explicit address-pointer materialization GCC-folded back into single combined load

rejected_forms.form: v18_cos_first_use (vx/vz declared at top but assigned later)
rejected_forms.reason: decl-before-assign doesn't change LUID of assignments
pointer alias folds back to global address; identical RTL to direct &Judge+idx
duplicate of v13 essentially; base ptr folded
explicit named partial products force separate pseudos; regresses RA

rejected_forms.form: v22_call_inline (top-of-block decls, no init; assigned later)
rejected_forms.reason: K&R top decls (no init) same as v5_struct_access

rejected_forms.form: v23_neg_vx_local (s32 nvx = -vx; used in rz comp)
rejected_forms.reason: named -vx as separate local; combine.c folds negu into surrounding mult anyway
baseline confirmation — no semantic change
